compact_statement: The witness is the nearest neighbor that answers back: the shared_B carrier preserves B/slot, while the paired reverse_partner row supplies the returned C value by swapping B and C.

core_result.summary: The realized WXYZTI overlay has a paired local grammar. Each directed C-transition has one shared_B row and one reciprocal reverse_partner row. reverse_partner is the answering row: it preserves A and swaps B/C. shared_B preserves B/slot, and its target C is supplied by the paired reverse_partner row.

next_problem: Generate the 12 reciprocal shared_B/reverse_partner pairs from a native candidate universe, rather than merely auditing realized WXYZTI rows.

pair_coupling_law.interpretation: shared_B's target C is not explained by a standalone shared_B formula. It is coupled to the reciprocal reverse_partner row whose from_B/from_slot supplies the answer.
key_equalities: S and R share role_pair, from_C, to_C, C_delta, integer_C_delta, and lift_q

reverse_partner_law.interpretation: reverse_partner preserves A, sends to_C = from_B = from_slot, and sends to_B/to_slot = from_C.

shared_B_law.interpretation: shared_B preserves B/slot. No additional global linear invariant involving A/C was found in the tested family.

title: WXYZTI pair grammar checkpoint
